architecture_decision: Keep the SQLite CEO ledger and service-request chain as source of truth. Add goal-to-DAG, governance-policy, sandbox-boundary, browser-recovery, and trace/eval contracts before any dependency install or external worker start.

github_api_boundary.api_result: HTTP 403 rate limit exceeded for the selected repo metadata batch
github_api_boundary.fallback: Use accessible GitHub pages, GitHub topic/search snippets, and source URLs; preserve rate-limit boundary for later authenticated/current refresh.

purpose: Refresh current open-source and GitHub signals specifically for scalable agent execution governance: goal-to-DAG orchestration, policy control planes, computer/browser-use sandboxes, agent observability, and incident/SRE gates.

recommended_local_builds.why: Before launching many autonomous lane chats, define how one CEO goal becomes tasks, dependencies, assignees, checkpoints, and synthesis evidence.
Compare the existing approval/scope/authority/preflight chain with external governance-control-plane patterns.
Browser, shell, file, MCP, VSCode, and desktop-control surfaces must be isolated before external workers can act safely.
Browser automation should log recoveries, retries, observed state, and stop reasons before any approved public action worker exists.
Scale requires per-lane learning/eval loops, cost/safety monitoring, and trace export without immediately adopting a hosted backend.

rows.agent_company_fit: Closest external signal to the local service-worker gate chain already built.
rows.local_implication: Promote a policy-kernel comparison against current approval, scope, command-safety, authority, and preflight reports.
rows.name: cordum-io/cordum
rows.signal: GitHub topic listing describes an open agent control plane with pre-execution policy enforcement, approval gates, and audit trails.

rows.agent_company_fit: Direct pattern for turning user goals into lane tasks and manager dispatch graphs.
rows.local_implication: Add a local report-only task-DAG contract before launching many more lane-agent chats.
rows.name: open-multi-agent/open-multi-agent
rows.signal: Recent TypeScript-native framework that turns a goal into a task DAG, parallelizes independent work, and provides a post-run dashboard.

rows.agent_company_fit: Use as language and requirement baseline for CEO/manager/worker architecture.
rows.local_implication: The company needs a deterministic policy layer around dynamic agent planning, not just task prompts.
rows.name: GitHub: What is AI agent orchestration?
rows.signal: Defines agent orchestration as the control layer for managing execution, shared context, collaboration, guardrails, cost limits, and HITL checkpoints.

rows.agent_company_fit: Pattern for a future unified worker workspace where browser downloads, files, and code execution share one evidence boundary.
rows.local_implication: Extend the sandbox execution gate contract with browser/file/shell/MCP cross-contamination checks before any container start.
rows.name: agent-infra/sandbox
rows.signal: All-in-one agent sandbox combining browser, shell, file, MCP operations, VSCode Server, VNC, Jupyter, and terminal in one container.

rows.agent_company_fit: Pattern source for read-only browser workers and future approved public-action browser workers.
rows.local_implication: Keep browser workers behind exact-scope packets and add recovery-loop logging requirements.
rows.name: browser-use/browser-use
rows.signal: Browser Use 0.13 introduces a beta agent with Rust core, browser harness, persistent tools, and recovery loops inspired by coding agents.

rows.agent_company_fit: Pattern source for agent-team evaluation, reusable workflows, and runtime intelligence.
rows.local_implication: The local company needs eval/learning loops per lane before it scales worker count.
rows.name: NVIDIA/NeMo-Agent-Toolkit
rows.signal: Open-source toolkit for connecting and optimizing teams of AI agents, with instrumentation, observability, continuous learning, MCP publishing, and workflow UI ideas.

rows.agent_company_fit: Pattern source for trace export, evaluation, and cost monitoring without committing to a backend.
rows.local_implication: Promote a local trace-to-eval export contract before integrating Langfuse/Phoenix/MLflow/AgentOps.
rows.name: mlflow/mlflow
rows.signal: MLflow positions itself as an AI engineering platform for agents with debugging, evaluation, monitoring, OpenTelemetry, MCP, cost, and safety monitoring.

rows.agent_company_fit: Pattern source for manager routing across departments and lane-specific specialists.
rows.local_implication: Add explicit route-decision evidence rows whenever the CEO sends work to lane managers.
rows.name: 2FastLabs/agent-squad
rows.signal: Framework for routing among multiple agents and maintaining context across interactions.

rows.agent_company_fit: Pattern source for production incident response around autonomous workers.
rows.local_implication: Add local agent-SRE severity, rollback, and pause contracts before enabling worker starts.
rows.name: microsoft/agent-governance-toolkit
rows.signal: Search result highlights Agent SRE, OpenTelemetry, Prometheus, and integrations with observability platforms.

rows.agent_company_fit: Pattern source for lightweight browser-worker command boundaries.
rows.local_implication: Browser workers should have a CLI-command safety review similar to service-worker decision command safety.
rows.name: vercel-labs/agent-browser
rows.signal: New browser automation CLI for AI agents, described as a fast native Rust CLI.

rows.agent_company_fit: Longer-term pattern for high-risk desktop workers.
rows.local_implication: Desktop-control workers need the strictest service gate: screen recording, action allowlist, credential masking, and kill switch.
rows.name: trycua/cua
rows.signal: GitHub topic result describes open-source infrastructure for computer-use agents controlling full desktops across operating systems.

rows.agent_company_fit: Alternative sandbox-runtime pattern to compare against E2B and AIO Sandbox.
rows.local_implication: Sandbox selection should be delayed until the local boundary matrix and cost/secrets controls are complete.
rows.name: opensandbox-group/OpenSandbox
rows.signal: GitHub result describes a sandbox runtime for AI agents with SDK usage, integrations, browser automation, and training workloads.

rows.agent_company_fit: Practical safety pattern for local task workers.
rows.local_implication: Our sandbox gate should require path allowlists/blocklists, idle/total timeouts, and interactive-stdin rejection.
rows.name: auto-use/Auto-Use
rows.signal: Search result describes sandboxed Bash confined to a desktop workspace with path blocklists, timeouts, and stdin detection plus browser automation.
